data.title: Copper Fox announces $1.5M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Copper Fox Metals (TSXV:CUU:CA) on Thursday announced a non-brokered private placement consisting of up to 7,317,073 units at a price of $0.205 per unit, to raise up to $1.5 million.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one-half common share purchase warrant.
  • Each whole warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one common share for a two-year term, for an exercise price of $0.25 during the first 12-month period after the closing of the offering and $0.30 during the subsequent 12-month period after the closing of the offering.
  • The offering is available to all existing shareholders of Copper Fox who, as of the close of business on February 5, 2025, held shares.
  • The offering is expected to close by February 28, 2025.
  • TECK +1.3% premarket to $43.06
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BankGuam Holding Company approves $500,000 buyback program
data.description:
• BankGuam Holding Company (OTC:BKGM) on Thursday approved a share repurchase program worth up to $500,000 of its outstanding common stock.
  • The company may purchase shares through open market transactions, block purchases, and privately negotiated transactions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Spectrum Brands Non-GAAP EPS of $1.02 beats by $0.10, revenue of $700.2M misses by $3.11M
data.description:
• Spectrum Brands press release (NYSE:SPB): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.02 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $700.2M (+1.2% Y/Y) misses by $3.11M.
  • Maintaining Fiscal 2025 Earnings Framework and Continue to Expect Low Single-Digit Net Sales Growth and Mid to High Single-Digits Adjusted EBITDA Growth for Fiscal 2025, Targeting ~50% Conversion of Adjusted EBITDA to Adjusted Free Cash Flow

data.title: Eli Lilly in charts: Mounjaro, Zepbound, and Jardiance drive sales growth in Q4
Huntington Ingalls misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Huntington Ingalls press release (NYSE:HII): Q4 GAAP EPS of $3.15 misses by $0.38.
  • Revenue of $3B (-5.7% Y/Y) misses by $60M.
  • Backlog of $48.7 billion at year-end.

  • Net cash provided by operating activities in 2024 was $393 million and free cash flow was $40 million, compared to $970 million and $692 million, respectively, in 2023.

    New contract awards in 2024 were approximately $12.1 billion, bringing total backlog to approximately $48.7 billion as of December 31, 2024.

  • HII’s Financial Outlook1 includes the following expectations:

    • Mid to long term HII revenue growth of 4%+
    • Mid to long term shipbuilding revenue growth of approximately 4%
    • Mid to long term Mission Technologies revenue growth of approximately 5%
    • FY25 shipbuilding revenue between $8.9 and $9.1 billion; expect shipbuilding operating margin2 between 5.5% and 6.5%
    • FY25 Mission Technologies revenue between $2.9 to $3.1 billion, Mission Technologies segment operating margin2 between 4.0% and 4.5%; and Mission Technologies EBITDA margin2 between 8.0% and 8.5%
    • FY25 free cash flow2,3 between $300 and $500 million
    FY25 Outlook1
    Shipbuilding Revenue2 $8.9B - $9.1B
    Shipbuilding Operating Margin2 5.5% - 6.5%
    Mission Technologies Revenue $2.9B - $3.1B
    Mission Technologies Segment Operating Margin2 4.0% - 4.5%
    Mission Technologies EBITDA Margin2 8.0% - 8.5%
    Operating FAS/CAS Adjustment ($43M)
    Non-current State Income Tax Benefit/Expense4 ~$0M
    Interest Expense ($130M)
    Non-operating Retirement Benefit $191M
    Effective Tax Rate ~21%
    Depreciation & Amortization ~$340M
    Capital Expenditures ~4% of Sales
    Free Cash Flow $300M - $500M

data.title: Thermon Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.01, revenue of $134.4M misses by $4.51M
data.description:
• Thermon press release (NYSE:THR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.01.

  • New orders of $138.6 million, +11.4%; book-to-bill ratio of 1.03x

data.title: Altus Power to be acquired by TPG in deal valued at $2.2B
data.description:
Altus Power (NYSE:AMPS) +27.1% pre-market after saying it agreed to be acquired by TPG through its TPG Rise Climate investing platform for $5.00/share in cash, valuing the company at ~$2.2B including outstanding debt, confirming previous speculation.

The purchase price represents a 66% premium to Altus Power's (NYSE:AMPS) unaffected closing price on October 15, 2024, the last trading day before the announcement of a formal review of strategic alternatives by the board.

By combining TPG Rise Climate’s investment capabilities with Altus Power’s (AMPS) domain expertise in commercial-scale solar, Altus said it will "be able to scale its operations more rapidly to meet the surging demand for increased power generation."

data.title: MACOM Technology beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• MACOM Technology press release (NASDAQ:MTSI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $218.1M (+38.8% Y/Y) beats by $3.18M.

  • Business Outlook: For the fiscal second quarter ending April 4, 2025, MACOM expects revenue to be in the range of $227 million to $233 million vs. $223.59M consensus. Adjusted gross margin is expected to be between 57% and 58%, and adjusted earnings per diluted share is expected to be between $0.82 and $0.86 vs. $0.83 consensus, utilizing an anticipated non-GAAP income tax rate of 3% and 76.0 million fully diluted shares outstanding.

data.title: Colliers International Non-GAAP EPS of $2.26, revenue of $1.5B
data.description:
• Colliers International press release (NASDAQ:CIGI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.26.
  • Revenue of $1.5B (+21.0% Y/Y).
  • Outlook for 2025The Company expects growth in 2025 both internally and from completed acquisitions. On a consolidated basis, high single digit to low-teens percentage revenue growth and low-teens Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EPS growth are expected. The outlook reflects currently prevailing foreign exchange rates, which are closely tied to international trade uncertainty. The outlook drivers by segment are described in the accompanying earnings call presentation.

data.title: ConocoPhillips declares $0.78 dividend
data.description:
• ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) declares $0.78/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.11%
  • Payable March 3; for shareholders of record Feb. 17; ex-div Feb. 13.
  • See COP Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
